items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities including unrestricted file upload, incorrect authorization, and stored XSS, which could lead to arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, and security feature bypass.

items.title: File Upload Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.6) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to privilege escalation.

items.title: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: A critical improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.1) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files using a polyglot technique, potentially leading to remote code execution. Magento 2.4.9 contains the fix, but older versions remain vulnerable without backports.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This bulletin addresses multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read. Key CVEs include CVE-2026-21284 (Incorrect Authorization, Full System Takeover), CVE-2026-21285 (Improper Access Control, Data Leak), and CVE-2026-21310 (Stored XSS, Admin Hijacking).

items.title: Incorrect Authorization leading to Full System Takeover
items.summary: A critical incorrect authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that allows attackers to execute code remotely, leading to a full system takeover.

items.title: Improper Access Control leading to Data Leak
items.summary: A critical improper access control vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that bypasses security filters to access restricted information, leading to a data leak.

items.title: Stored XSS leading to Admin Hijacking
items.summary: A critical stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source where malicious scripts can escalate user privileges, potentially leading to admin hijacking.

items.title: Improper Input Validation leading to Account Takeover
items.summary: This critical vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.1), also known as SessionReaper, allows attackers to take over customer accounts via the Commerce REST API due to improper input validation. Adobe is aware of active exploitation in the wild.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This regularly scheduled security update resolves critical and important vulnerabilities, including security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution. It specifically addresses CVE-2025-47110 (Stored XSS via Server-Side Template Injection) and a reflected XSS vulnerability (VULN-31547) with a one-click account takeover issue.

items.title: Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via Server-Side Template Injection
items.summary: A critical stored XSS vulnerability in Adobe Commerce that allows attackers to inject malicious scripts via server-side template injection.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This critical security update addresses vulnerabilities including Improper Limitation of a Pathname, Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF), Improper Access Control, and Insufficiently Protected Credentials, which could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and application denial-of-service.

items.title: Security update for Adobe Commerce, Magento Open Source and Adobe Commerce Webhooks Plugin
items.summary: This update resolves critical and important vulnerabilities, including CVE-2024-34102 (CosmicSting), which is a critical bug (CVSS 9.8) allowing attackers to read files, steal crypt keys, and potentially achieve remote code execution. Adobe is aware that CVE-2024-34102 has been exploited in the wild.

items.title: Improper Access Control vulnerability in Magento
items.summary: Adobe Commerce versions 2.4.7-beta1, 2.4.7-p3, 2.4.6-p8, 2.4.5-p10, 2.4.4-p11 and earlier are affected by an Improper Access Control vulnerability that could result in Privilege escalation. A low-privileged attacker could leverage this vulnerability to bypass security measures and gain unauthorized access to modify limited fields.
